interpretation: An evaluator-side Bayesian latent change-point decoder executed a pre-ranked, phase-invariant cycle of public complete-recipe experiments. It received no phase-change signal and selected exactly one absolute, same-action temporal, or fit-ranked cross-action relational likelihood channel per experiment. Transition-versus-stable semantics were selected from each reference's establishment time under the frozen post-change experiment budget.

policy.action_selection: derive same-condition relation groups from every declared material intervention; select one fit-ranked realizable group per declaration; fill the four-action policy set using predictive-fit-only information; execute the final set in canonical order without phase input
policy.evidence_policy: select eight whole public-summary blocks per evidence channel from predictive-fit samples only; use exactly one absolute, same-action temporal, or cross-action relational likelihood channel per experiment; choose transition versus stable semantics from the latent change-point hypothesis and reference age; never expose phase, reset, or hidden truth
policy.reference_policy: retain each selected action's first observation and global experiment index; a cross-action relation may cold-start an action only while that action has no same-action reference; all later evidence for that action uses its own temporal reference
